item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

media:content.media:title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss
media:content.media:description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

item.title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss

item.dc:creator: Matt Dixon
item.description: President Donald Trump isn’t on the ballot this year, but with roughly six months before Election Day, he’ll still likely be the biggest factor in determining the fortunes of the Republican Party

media:content.media:text: President Trump Hosts Small Business Summit
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during a small-business summit in the East Room of the White House on May 4.

item.title: What GOP officials are hopeful — and worried — about six months from the midterms

item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

media:content.media:title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss
media:content.media:description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

item.title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss

item.dc:creator: Brennan Leach
Melanie Zanona
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s political career is at stake on Saturday as Louisiana voters weigh in on the Republican for the first time since he voted to convict President Donald Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial

media:content.media:credit: Getty Images; Bloomberg via Getty Images; AP
media:content.media:text: Rep. Julia Letlow, Sen. Bill Cassidy, and Louisiana state Treasurer John Fleming.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Julia Letlow, Sen. Bill Cassidy, and Louisiana state Treasurer John Fleming.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy faces a primary for the first time since his Trump impeachment vote

item.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, former FBI Director James Comey says Todd Blanche should brush up on legal rules after the acting attorney general cited 11 months of evidence in the DOJ's indictment of Comey.

media:content.media:title: James Comey urges Todd Blanche to ‘bone up’ on legal rules amid indictment: Full interview
media:content.media:description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, former FBI Director James Comey says Todd Blanche should brush up on legal rules after the acting attorney general cited 11 months of evidence in the DOJ's indictment of Comey.

item.title: James Comey urges Todd Blanche to ‘bone up’ on legal rules amid indictment: Full interview

item.dc:creator: Shira Pinson
Matt Bradley
item.description: Calls for Jewish settlements in Gaza are shifting toward the mainstream in Israel, with some groups advocating the wholesale ethnic cleansing of Palestinians.

media:content.media:text: Image: Israelis Observe Yom Ha'atzmaut, National Independence Day
media:content.media:description: Right-wing Nachala movement settlers march near the Gaza border on April 22, 2026 near Kibbutz Nir Am, Israel.

media:content.media:text: PALESTINIAN-ISRAEL-CONFLICT-SETTLEMENTS
media:content.media:description: Israel's Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich, center, surrounded by Israeli settlers at the end of the resettlement ceremony of Sa-Nur, south of Jenin, in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Apr. 19.

media:content.media:text: Daily life of displaced Palestinians among rubbles of the Islamic University in Gaza
media:content.media:description: Displaced Palestinian families live in makeshift tents and damaged buildings within the campus of the Islamic University of Gaza, which was destroyed by Israeli attacks, pictured on April 12.

media:content.media:text: Israelis Observe Yom Ha'atzmaut, National Independence Day
media:content.media:description: Nachala movement leader Daniella Weiss gives a speech near Kibbutz Nir Am, Israel, on Apr. 22.

item.title: Extremist Jewish settlers eye Gaza as they seek to expel Palestinians from enclave

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the nation’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.“I want the people who are being affected by this, who are in quarantine or who have loved ones who are in quarantine, to rest assured that they are in the best of ha

media:content.media:text: Carl Goldman in quaratine at UNMC.
media:content.media:description: U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius arrive Monday at the University of Nebraska Medical Center’s Davis Global Center in Omaha.

media:content.media:text: The exterior of Emory University Hospital
media:content.media:description: U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius arrive Monday at the University of Nebraska Medical Center’s Davis Global Center in Omaha.

media:content.media:text: Jake Rosmarin shows off his quarantine room.
media:content.media:description: U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius arrive Monday at the University of Nebraska Medical Center’s Davis Global Center in Omaha.

media:content.media:text: Nebraska Medicine's Davis Global Center in Omaha, Neb. where American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship will quarantine.
media:content.media:description: U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius arrive Monday at the University of Nebraska Medical Center’s Davis Global Center in Omaha.

media:content.media:text: The biocontainment unit at UNMC.
media:content.media:description: U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius arrive Monday at the University of Nebraska Medical Center’s Davis Global Center in Omaha.

item.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., on Sunday called for more U.S. military action in Iran, saying the ongoing stalemate in the war is “hurting us all.”

media:content.media:text: Congressional Lawmakers Work To Find Common Ground On DHS Funding As Shutdown Deadline Looms
media:content.media:description: U.S. Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC) walks through the halls of the U.S. Capitol on Feb., 12, 2026 in Washington, DC.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham says status quo in the Strait of Hormuz is ‘hurting us all’

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: Mustafe Denbil runs an agricultural shop and farm in northern Somalia, but three months after ordering fertilizer and pesticides, the supplies his business needs are still stranded in Dubai

media:content.media:text: Humanitarian crisis deepens in Somalia after Strait of Hormuz closure
media:content.media:description: Residents of the Maacani Camp in Mogadishu, Somalia are struggling to survive in tents under limited conditions as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz cause serious disruptions in the passage of ships on May 5.

item.title: How the Middle East conflict is worsening Somalia’s slide into famine

item.dc:creator: Denise Chow
item.description: SpaceX plans to launch a new version of its Starship rocket — a prototype of the system that NASA hopes will carry its astronauts to the moon — on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Starship V3 on the launch pad during a wet dress rehearsal.
media:content.media:description: Starship V3 on the launchpad during a rehearsal.

item.title: For SpaceX, the stakes of next week’s Starship rocket test flight are sky-high

item.dc:creator: Mustafa Fattah
item.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

media:content.media:text: Photo illustrations of maggots alongside surgical equipment
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Polly Cleveland
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Medicinal maggots in a sachet.
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Medicinal maggots in a petri dish.
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

item.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Gabriella Rudy
item.description: New York officials urged commuters to work from home Monday as a strike by Long Island Rail Road workers threatened to disrupt travel for hundreds of thousands of riders across the region.Gov.

media:content.media:text: Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside
media:content.media:description: LIRR employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People exit a Long Island Rail Road train cart inside of a station
media:content.media:description: LIRR employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

item.title: New York braces for chaotic Monday commute with Long Island Rail Road strike

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Three people were killed and several others were injured after a driver crashed into multiple cars and pedestrians late Saturday night in Oakland, California, authorities said

media:content.media:text: A close up view of an Oakland Police patch on the shoulder of a uniform
media:content.media:description: A patch for the Oakland Police Department during a regular season game between the Chicago White Sox and Oakland Athletics on July 2, 2023, at RingCentral Coliseum in Oakland, Calif.

item.title: 3 people killed, several others injured after driver crashes into crowd in Oakland, California

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
Cristian Santana
item.description: A woman gave birth in a Brooklyn courtroom late Friday night, just hours after being discharged from a hospital, according to police and witnesses

media:content.media:text: The exterior of Kings County Criminal Court as seen from street view on Google Maps
media:content.media:description: Kings County Criminal Court in Brooklyn, New York City.

item.title: Woman gives birth ‘on courtroom bench’ during arraignment in Brooklyn

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Through films like “Blazing Saddles” and “Spaceballs” and a pioneering career on television and Broadway, Mel Brooks has had a lifetime of serving up the funny.

media:content.media:text: Mel Brooks smiles and waves on the red carpet
media:content.media:description: Mel Brooks.

item.title: Decades of comedy gold: Mel Brooks donates document archive to National Comedy Center

item.dc:creator: Mirna Alsharif
Hannah Peart
item.description: The MV Hondius cruise ship is due to arrive Monday at the Netherlands’ Port of Rotterdam, where the remaining crew will disembark and the vessel will be disinfected, according to ship owner Oceanwide Expeditions

media:content.media:text: This aerial picture shows a general view of the cruise ship MV Hondius stationary off the port of Praia
media:content.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on May 5.

item.title: MV Hondius cruise ship to arrive in the Netherlands on Monday

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Former FBI Director James Comey said Sunday that he has “complete faith in our judicial system” as he faces an ongoing federal case over a 2025 Instagram post

media:content.media:text: James Comey In Conversation With Nicolle Wallace
media:content.media:description: Former FBI Director James Comey In Conversation With MSNBC's Nicolle Wallace at 92NY on May 30, 2023 in New York.

item.title: James Comey says he has ‘complete faith’ in the judicial system amid seashell case

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: Only the Taiwanese people can decide their future, Taiwan’s president said ‌Sunday, after President Donald Trump appeared to raise doubts about long-standing U.S. policy on his trip to China

media:content.media:text: U.S. President Trump Meets With China's President Xi And Attends State Banquet
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump reviews an honor guard with Chinese President Xi Jinping on May 14.

media:content.media:text: TAIWAN-US-CHINA-DEFENCE
media:content.media:description: Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te speaks with AFP in Taipei in February.

item.title: Taiwan gently pushes back on Trump’s warnings after China summit

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the nation’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.“I want the people who are being affected by this, who are in quarantine or who have loved ones who are in quarantine, to rest assured that they are in the best of ha

media:content.media:text: Carl Goldman in quaratine at UNMC.
media:content.media:description: U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius arrive Monday at the University of Nebraska Medical Center’s Davis Global Center in Omaha.

media:content.media:text: The exterior of Emory University Hospital
media:content.media:description: U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius arrive Monday at the University of Nebraska Medical Center’s Davis Global Center in Omaha.

media:content.media:text: Jake Rosmarin shows off his quarantine room.

media:content.media:text: Nebraska Medicine's Davis Global Center in Omaha, Neb. where American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship will quarantine.

item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., on Sunday called for more U.S. military action in Iran, saying the ongoing stalemate in the war is “hurting us all.”

media:content.media:text: Congressional Lawmakers Work To Find Common Ground On DHS Funding As Shutdown Deadline Looms
media:content.media:description: U.S. Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC) walks through the halls of the U.S. Capitol on Feb., 12, 2026 in Washington, DC.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham says status quo in the Strait of Hormuz is ‘hurting us all’

item.dc:creator: Reuters
item.description: Thai police have charged ​a train driver with negligence after a crash on Saturday in central Bangkok in which a freight train collided with a public bus.

media:content.media:text: Thailand Train Crash
media:content.media:description: Investigators examine the site of a train crash near an airport rail link station in Bangkok, Thailand, on May 16.

item.title: Train driver charged after crash in Bangkok, Thailand kills 8

item.dc:creator: Reuters
item.description: At least four people were killed, including three in the Moscow region, after Ukraine launched its biggest overnight drone attack on the Russian capital in more ​than a year, local officials said on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Residents clear broken windows in a damaged apartment building, as seen from the outside of the building
media:content.media:description: Residents clear broken windows in a damaged apartment building following a Ukrainian drone attack in Krasnogorsk, Moscow, on Sunday.

item.title: Ukraine drones kill four in Russia, Moscow faces biggest attack in over a year

item.dc:creator: Mustafa Fattah
item.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

media:content.media:text: Photo illustrations of maggots alongside surgical equipment
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Polly Cleveland
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Medicinal maggots in a sachet.
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

media:content.media:text: Medicinal maggots in a petri dish.
media:content.media:description: The thinking behind maggot therapy is straightforward: Dying tissue must be removed from wounds in order to prevent infection. To maggots, this dead tissue is food.

item.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.

item.dc:creator: Denise Chow
item.description: SpaceX plans to launch a new version of its Starship rocket — a prototype of the system that NASA hopes will carry its astronauts to the moon — on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Starship V3 on the launch pad during a wet dress rehearsal.
media:content.media:description: Starship V3 on the launchpad during a rehearsal.

item.title: For SpaceX, the stakes of next week’s Starship rocket test flight are sky-high
